title: What Can Neural Networks Reason About?
llm_reason: Introduces the highly influential algorithmic alignment framework, which provides the theoretical foundation for how neural networks and Graph Neural Networks simulate algorithms and reason about them.

title: Neural Execution of Graph Algorithms
llm_reason: A foundational method paper that popularized neural algorithmic reasoning (NAR) by training GNNs to mimic classical graph algorithms step-by-step.

title: Neural algorithmic reasoning
llm_reason: The definitive position paper that formally defines and establishes the field of Neural Algorithmic Reasoning, linking deep learning with classical algorithms.

title: The clrs algorithmic reasoning benchmark
llm_reason: Introduces the CLRS algorithmic reasoning benchmark, which has become the standard benchmark used by almost all subsequent research in this field.

title: Graph neural networks are dynamic programmers
llm_reason: Provides a deep theoretical bridge connecting Graph Neural Networks and dynamic programming via category theory, establishing a rigorous mathematical basis for algorithmic alignment.

title: Dual Algorithmic Reasoning
llm_reason: An interesting method paper that introduces primal-dual formulation learning to improve generalization in neural algorithmic reasoning.

title: Neural algorithmic reasoning with causal regularisation
llm_reason: Proposes Hint-ReLIC, enhancing out-of-distribution generalization in NAR using causal regularization on intermediate trajectories.

title: Transformers meet neural algorithmic reasoners
llm_reason: Proposes a hybrid architecture (TransNAR) combining Transformers and GNNs, addressing a key intersection of language models and algorithmic reasoners.

title: On the Markov Property of Neural Algorithmic Reasoning: Analyses and Methods
llm_reason: Addresses the Markov property of algorithmic reasoning by proposing ForgetNet to improve validation on the CLRS benchmark.

title: Simulation of Graph Algorithms with Looped Transformers
llm_reason: A highly valuable theoretical analysis proving that looped transformers can simulate standard graph algorithms with size-independent parameters.

title: Deep equilibrium algorithmic reasoning
llm_reason: Introduces an equilibrium-based approach (DEQ) to neural algorithmic reasoning, moving away from step-by-step recurrence.

title: Open-book neural algorithmic reasoning
llm_reason: Proposes an open-book retrieval-augmented method for algorithmic reasoning, facilitating multi-task generalization.

title: Puzzles: A benchmark for neural algorithmic reasoning
llm_reason: Introduces PUZZLES, a high-quality evaluation benchmark focusing on logic and reinforcement learning within algorithmic reasoning.

title: Transformers can do arithmetic with the right embeddings
llm_reason: A highly cited and foundational method for improving arithmetic reasoning in transformers using relative digit position embeddings (Abacus Embeddings).

title: Understanding transformer reasoning capabilities via graph algorithms
llm_reason: Provides a rigorous, foundational analysis of transformer scaling regimes and representational capabilities when solving graph algorithms.

title: Nar-* icp: Neural execution of classical icp-based pointcloud registration algorithms
llm_reason: A highly specialized domain application of neural algorithmic reasoning to point-cloud registration (ICP).

title: Tackling gnarly problems: Graph neural algorithmic reasoning reimagined through reinforcement learning
llm_reason: Reformulates NAR as an RL problem (GNARL) to solve NP-hard problems without relying on step-by-step supervision from expert algorithms.

title: Discrete Neural Algorithmic Reasoning
llm_reason: Proposes decoupling discrete and continuous data flows for perfect out-of-distribution algorithmic generalization.

title: Primal-Dual Neural Algorithmic Reasoning
llm_reason: Develops a primal-dual bipartite GNN framework targeting approximation algorithms for combinatorial problems.

title: KNARsack: Teaching Neural Algorithmic Reasoners to Solve Pseudo-Polynomial Problems
llm_reason: A specific method paper applying dynamic programming supervision to the pseudo-polynomial Knapsack problem.

title: Leveraging Classical Algorithms for Graph Neural Networks
llm_reason: A useful study showing that pretraining GNNs on CLRS algorithmic tasks transfers algorithmic inductive biases to molecular property prediction.

title: Recurrent Aggregators in Neural Algorithmic Reasoning
llm_reason: A specialized methodology replacing equivariant aggregators with recurrent networks to improve performance on ordered tasks like sorting.

title: Tropical attention: Neural algorithmic reasoning for combinatorial algorithms
llm_reason: Introduces Tropical Attention, a geometrically-grounded mechanism to enhance algorithmic reasoning for NP-hard problems.
